title: The Morality of Gendou
wikitext:
[]: The Morality of Gendou             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Dec 20, 2009 6:20 pm From the thread "Gendou's Day Off", someone mentioned that most people here dislike Gendou's actions in a serious context. So I was wondering what people here really thought about him.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun Dec 20, 2009 6:40 pm I think Commander Gendou is a pretty cool guy. eh kills angels and doesn't afraid of anything. ...nah but really Gendo's a pretty complicated character. All of his actions seem pretty on the money considering the situation... until you realise he's got a hard-on for a 14yo angel-wife-clone and wants to doom humantity so he can have chits-chats with his "dead" wife.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sun Dec 20, 2009 7:09 pm skikes wrote: I think Commander Gendou is a pretty cool guy. eh kills angels and doesn't afraid of anything. ...nah but really Gendo's a pretty complicated character. All of his actions seem pretty on the money considering the situation... until you realise he's got a hard-on for a 14yo angel-wife-clone and wants to doom humantity so he can have chits-chats with his "dead" wife.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Sun Dec 20, 2009 7:17 pm I think that he is a bad person. He ruins people's lives just so he can be with Yui again. He used Ritsuko and Naoko as sex dolls and doesn't care about them, he shuns Shinji and uses him and Rei as pawns for his scenario, knowingly doomed the people at the South Pole during Second Impact, Tanged people without their consent, and essentially destroyed civilization for his own selfish desire for Yui. He will go to any means to see Yui again, even if that means killing people and destroying peoples' lives.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:04 pm DIDDY wrote: He wanted to doom humanity? How? Gendo (ep. 25): "No, it's not that we are returning to nothingness . We are only restoring everything to its original state. We are only returning to our mother, who has been lost to this world. All souls will become one and find eternal peace . That is all there is to it."

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:09 pm skikes wrote: Sammaeloo wrote: He used Ritsuko and Naoko as sex dolls

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:31 pm Sammaeloo wrote: It seems that in a relationship he only wanted sex from them.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sun Dec 20, 2009 8:54 pm skikes wrote: ... If everyone you knew told you the world is round and one crazy dude told the world is flat which one are you gonna trust?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:01 pm DIDDY wrote: When it comes to morality, yes, actions are relevant. But so are intentions.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:09 pm Sammaeloo wrote: It seems that in a relationship he only wanted sex from them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 20, 2009 9:18 pm His actions are reprehensible, but I imagine Gendo to have good intentions. He's no less moral than any other character; he's merely not complying with society's expectations and being exactly the sort of dick everyone else in the series is deep down.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Sun Dec 20, 2009 10:10 pm Gendo is a man given in to despair. He has been so devastated by his wife's death that right and wrong don't matter to him anymore. All he cares about is being with his wife again/flipping off God(I think he says as much in the director's cut of the Platinum Edition)/returning to the perfect unity of the womb via Instrumentality. Also, he wasn't using Naoko or Ritsuko for sex, he seduced them and used them to further his aims. You know how in The Producers, Bialystock has sex with old women for the money to produce his plays, well Gendo has sex with women for the SCIENCE! to produce Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Dec 20, 2009 10:47 pm Gendo is not the one-dimensional villain most people seem to take him for. He is as complicated and multifaceted as anyone else in the series. It’s just that most of the time all we see is the coldness he hides behind, so we come to think that’s all there is to him. Gendo isn’t painted in black and white, but in shades of gray. Just like anybody else. Also, I sick of the “good” doctors getting a free pass all the time. Yes, Gendo did use them, but with their full and knowing consent. Don’t try and treat them as hapless victims just because they’re female.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Sun Dec 20, 2009 11:59 pm child of Lilith wrote: [Gendo] is as complicated and multifaceted as anyone else in the series. It’s just that most of the time all we see is the coldness he hides behind [...]

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:15 am Sammaeloo wrote: It seems that in a relationship he only wanted sex from them.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:25 am chaosakita wrote: Sammaeloo wrote: It seems that in a relationship he only wanted sex from them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 21, 2009 12:43 am Yea, not all guys are insatiable pussy-hunters. You should probably stop making sex-related generalizations.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Mon Dec 21, 2009 1:06 am AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, not all guys are insatiable pussy-hunters. You should probably stop making sex-related generalizations.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Dec 21, 2009 3:27 pm DIDDY wrote: child of Lilith wrote: [Gendo] is as complicated and multifaceted as anyone else in the series. It’s just that most of the time all we see is the coldness he hides behind [...]

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Mon Dec 21, 2009 4:54 pm Gendo is sort of like Golem, eaten alive by his own obssession. Actually a better comparison would be to say that Gendo is Batman gone wrong. They are both motivated by grief, they are both crazy awesome, but whereas Batman seeks to save the world, Gendo seeks to destroy it and recreate it. Actually, Gendo's motivation most reminds me of Satan from Paradise Lost. Also, no one here is apologizing for Ritsuko and Naoko. They are both monsters, but they are enthralled to another monster, Gendo. All three characters have multiple facets but at the end of the day, they chose to be monstrous.
